name: AppZen
oneliner: AppZen is the agentic AI orchestration layer for the enterprise CFO office — AI agents that connect and autonomously run finance processes (T&E, AP, procurement) across borders, languages, and ERP systems.
finserv: Customers include Amazon, Salesforce, JP Morgan Chase, Airbus, and Databricks. $290M total raised. The company works directly within SAP, Oracle, and other ERP environments — deeply embedded in regulated enterprise finance stacks. JP Morgan Chase as a customer signals institutional financial services validation.
team: Founded 2012 by CEO and CTO Kunal Verma. $180M Series D led by Riverwood Capital (September 2025). $290M lifetime. Profitable trajectory implied by growth round structure.
thesis: AppZen is the most enterprise-validated agentic finance company in the market — it already has JP Morgan Chase as a customer and is pitching itself as an autonomous alternative to BPO outsourcing for finance teams. The Agent Studio (December 2025) letting enterprises build custom finance agents from SOPs is a direct move into the territory where regulated infrastructure matters most. This gives you a benchmark competitor and potential integration partner: enterprises that run their finance ops on AppZen agents will need authenticated, compliant execution rails underneath. AppZen validates the market thesis while defining where the infrastructure gap sits below the application layer.
evidence: PRNewswire: AppZen Launches AI Agent Studio
